transcript_previews.transcript: I need an e-mail that I can send some sworn statements to you directly. I'm telling him I'm a friend of Mr. Soros's and that I am contacting him about Jelaine Maxwell and some organized crime within the Department of Justice office. Thank you.

My name is . I was referred to you by . A lady came to me who claims to have information on the Epstein case, and I'm trying to get an inroad to get her hooked up with the Southern District of New York, what's happening with the criminal prosecution. Can you give me a call at . If I don't answer, leave me a message. This is my cell. I'll get right back to you as soon as possible. Thank you.

Hello, how are you? My name is Tatiana Gooden. My number is... I see that you are the Assistant U.S. Attorney assigned to the Jeffrey Epstein case. I have some information which might be pertinent. If you could please return my call at your earliest convenience. Thank you.

From ABC News, I'm Sherry Preston. He's already a registered sex offender. Now, billionaire financier Jeffrey Epstein is accused of abusing dozens of girls. From New York, ABC's Aaron Katursky. The defense said the indictment amounts to a do-over for the feds, who allowed Jeffrey Epstein to plead to lesser charges a decade ago and arrange a non-prosecution agreement. They would never be daunted by such a specious document. Former federal prosecutor Rachel Maiman, who's now a partner at Lowenstein-Sandler, said Epstein's prior plea is an admission of guilt. I think they'll be able to use his pleas against him, and that is really good evidence. Prosecutors also said they found at Epstein's home here a vast trove of nude photos of females, many of whom appeared to be underage. Aaron Katursky, ABC News, New York. A guilty plea years ago involved then U.S. Attorney Alex Acosta, now President Trump's Labor Secretary. Speaker of the House, as Acosta needs to step down.
